route.name: Seattle (SeaTac)
route.sub: 1 night · Courtyard by Marriott Seattle SeaTac Airport

blocks.title: Alaska Airlines AS 305 - Nonstop - 6h42m
blocks.detail: MIA -> SEA, Economy, 4 passengers. CONFIRMED -- confirmation code UUKHNQ. Seats: Roque Corona 19A, Jennifer Borges 19B, Roque Luis Corona 19C, Chloe Corona 19D (whole family together in one row).
blocks.notes: Booked and confirmed (confirmation UUKHNQ). Per Alaska's actual payment summary: 70,000 Atmos Rewards points total (17,500/passenger) + $22.40 cash total ($5.60/passenger in taxes/fees), charged to the Visa ending 6730 and deducted from Atmos Rewards account ending 8720. This corrects the earlier cart estimate of 50,000 miles + $24 cash -- the actual charge came in 20,000 points higher than that estimate. Note: Alaska's loyalty program is now called Atmos Rewards (rebranded from Mileage Plan).

blocks.title: Deplane & walk to terminal
blocks.detail: A family of four boarding a full flight rarely deplanes first — budgeting the full walk from gate to the terminal food area, not just doorway-to-jetbridge time.

blocks.title: Lunch — Lil Woody's, SEA Airport (N Gates)
blocks.detail: Order-at-counter burgers & shakes near Gate N8 in the North Satellite -- Alaska's home concourse at SEA, and where AS305 has recently been arriving (e.g. Gate N6). Eating airside before baggage claim so there's no separate lunch stop after reaching the car. Quick-casual format fits the ~35-minute window; walk from a nearby N-gate is a few minutes at most.
blocks.notes: Estimate: 2 signature burgers ($11 each, e.g. Big Woody) + 2 kids meals ($6 each, e.g. plain Lil Woody or chicken strips & fries) = $34.00 subtotal, + SeaTac 10.4% tax ($3.54) + 10% tip on subtotal ($3.40) = $40.94 total. Drinks/shakes/fries not included. Priced from Lil Woody's published menu (their neighborhood locations) -- the SEA airport location's own posted pricing wasn't itemized online, and airport quick-service concessions commonly run somewhat higher, so treat this as a floor rather than exact.

blocks.title: Baggage claim
blocks.detail: Domestic arrival, no customs. Shorter wait than a typical heavy-travel-day estimate since the ~35 min terminal meal already gave bags time to reach the carousel.

blocks.title: Walk to the shuttle curb (baggage claim exit)
blocks.detail: Turo pickup for this host isn't at the official rental car facility — coordinate the exact meeting spot in-app, but plan on the common airport shuttle to a nearby off-airport lot.

blocks.title: Wait for / ride shuttle to Turo pickup location
blocks.detail: Same shuttle system used for off-airport rental lots — buses run every ~5-15 min, ride itself is ~5-8 min.

blocks.title: Turo pickup — 2024 Nissan Rogue
blocks.detail: Meet the host (Indrawati — 5.0★, All-Star Host, 287 trips), walk-around inspection, paperwork, load luggage, install the 2 child seats. Budgeting longer than a counter-rental pickup since it's an in-person host meet, not a kiosk.
blocks.notes: $561.31 total, confirmed (Reservation 59466401): 8 days @ $61.25/day less 15% week-long discount, + 2 child safety seats ($10/day each, included/confirmed), + $10 trip fee + $54.81 tax. 1,200 mi included (150 mi/day); $0.35/mi over. No payment due today — auto-charges to VISA ...9612 on Dec 12, 2026. Vehicle confirmed AWD with snow tires (host-listed feature). Snow chains are NOT included in this booking — available as a $35/trip add-on through the host, or bring/rent your own; worth deciding before the Dec 21 pass drive given how snowy this week runs historically.
blocks.flag: Return is Dec 26 by 4:00 PM at SEA — the itinerary previously assumed a Dec 27 morning drop-off; Day 7/8 have been restructured to match.

blocks.title: Drive to Grand Hyatt Seattle
blocks.detail: ~13-14 mi from the airport into downtown; budgeting a full 30 min for a Saturday afternoon with some traffic.

blocks.title: Arrive at hotel — bags held at bell desk
blocks.detail: Grand Hyatt Seattle's confirmed check-in time is 4:00 PM (confirmation #59319732) -- early check-in is not guaranteed. Bell desk holds the luggage while the room finishes prep.

blocks.title: Free time near the hotel (lobby/lounge)
blocks.detail: Waiting out the gap to the confirmed 4 PM check-in -- lobby, Hyatt Globalist club access if available, or a short walk nearby. Not a preview of Pike Place, which is Day 2's anchor.

blocks.title: Check in — Grand Hyatt Seattle
blocks.detail: 2 nights (Sat-Sun). Room 2 Double Beds, confirmation #59319732. World of Hyatt Globalist doesn't guarantee early check-in as a stated benefit, so 4 PM is the reliable time -- worth requesting room-ready notification via the Hyatt app on arrival day in case it's ready sooner.
blocks.notes: 15,000 pts/night x 2 nights = 30,000 total (confirmed, matches the reservation exactly). The $58 paid amount here is the $29/night destination fee, NOT covered by points -- billed separately to the card on file. Self-parking is $37/night in the adjacent garage (valet is $68/night with in/out privileges) -- budget ~$74 more for 2 nights of self-parking, confirm in/out access is included. Globalist perk includes complimentary breakfast both mornings (confirmed) -- used on Day 2 and Day 3 instead of paying for breakfast out.
blocks.cancelBy: Free cancel: contact hotel (no explicit deadline published)
blocks.flag: Check-in guaranteed 4:00 PM only — not earlier. $58 destination fee billed separately, not covered by points.

blocks.title: Freshen up & rest at the hotel
blocks.detail: Only 45 minutes of in-room decompression before heading to dinner -- a real compression from what a 2:25 PM check-in would have allowed, since the confirmed check-in is 4 PM. If the family would rather have more rest time, Cactus dinner could shift to 6-7 PM instead (tradeoff: later bedtime after an already-long travel day with a 3-hour timezone shift).

blocks.title: Drive to Cactus, South Lake Union
blocks.detail: ~1 mi — now driving since the car is already in hand.

blocks.title: Dinner — Cactus, South Lake Union
blocks.detail: Southwestern/Tex-Mex, confirmed kids menu, open until 10 PM.
blocks.notes: Green Enchilada $18.50 + Chimayo Enchilada $19 (adults) + Kid Taco $9 + Kid Quesadilla $9 (kids). Entrees only, no apps/drinks/dessert. Subtotal $55.50 + Seattle 10.55% tax ($5.86) + 20% tip on subtotal ($11.10) = $72.46 total.

blocks.title: Drive back to Grand Hyatt Seattle, park
Wind down & bedtime routine at the hotel
blocks.detail: Early night given the 3-hour body-clock jump and a full Seattle day tomorrow.

days.title: Seattle — Pike Place & Bainbridge Island ferry day

blocks.title: Breakfast — Grand Hyatt Seattle (included)
blocks.detail: Globalist complimentary breakfast.

blocks.title: Walk to Pike Place Market
blocks.detail: ~0.7 mi from the Grand Hyatt.

blocks.title: Pike Place Market
blocks.detail: Gum Wall, Post Alley, the fish-throwing at Pike Place Fish, Rachel the piggy bank, browsing the craft stalls, a pastry stop at Le Panier — the morning's anchor activity.

blocks.title: Walk to Colman Dock (Pier 52)
blocks.detail: Downhill from the market along the waterfront, ~0.7 mi.

blocks.title: Ticket line & wait for boarding — Colman Dock
blocks.detail: Walk-on tickets purchased at the terminal; buffer before boarding since Washington State Ferries' winter Seattle–Bainbridge schedule for this date isn't published yet as of mid-2026 — worth reconfirming the exact sailing time closer to the trip.

blocks.title: Ferry — Seattle to Bainbridge Island
blocks.detail: Washington State Ferries, ~35-minute crossing across Elliott Bay and Puget Sound.
blocks.notes: Walk-on fare is charged westbound only; the return leg is free. $11.35/adult x2 + free for both kids (under 19) + 3% card-payment surcharge (effective March 2026) = $23.38 round trip for the family.

blocks.title: Walk to Harbour Public House
blocks.detail: Along the Eagle Harbor waterfront from the ferry terminal.

blocks.title: Lunch — Harbour Public House, 231 Parfitt Way SW
blocks.detail: Bainbridge's neighbourhood pub, waterfront deck overlooking Eagle Harbor, real menu variety.
blocks.notes: Alaskan Cod Fish & Chips $19 + Pub Smash Burger with Cheese $16 (adults) + 2x Macaroni & Cheese Bowl $13 (kids — no separate kids menu, these are the best kid-appropriate items). Entrees only. Subtotal $61.00 + mandatory pre-tax 5% kitchen service charge ($3.05) + Bainbridge Island 9.2% sales tax on subtotal+service charge ($5.89) + 20% discretionary tip on subtotal only, per the menu's own guidance not to tip on the service charge or tax ($12.20) = $82.14 total.

blocks.title: Winslow Waterfront Park, downtown Winslow & Mora Iced Creamery
blocks.detail: Playground and lawn at Waterfront Park, browsing the small shops along Winslow Way, treat stop at Mora Iced Creamery — all an easy flat walk from the pub, no car needed.

blocks.title: Walk back to the ferry terminal
Ferry — Bainbridge Island to Seattle
blocks.detail: Return crossing, no fare (eastbound leg is free).

blocks.title: Relax at the hotel
blocks.detail: A real, unhurried block after a full day of walking and two ferry crossings — no more sightseeing stacked on today.

blocks.title: Walk to Elephant & Castle
blocks.detail: 1415 5th Ave — a few blocks from the Grand Hyatt.

blocks.title: Dinner — Elephant & Castle, 1415 5th Ave
blocks.detail: British pub beneath the Motif Hotel, a few blocks from the Grand Hyatt — real menu variety (fish & chips, burgers, pies) and a dedicated printed Kids menu with kids drink included. Open until midnight on Sundays, comfortable buffer after a 6:30 seating.
blocks.notes: Fish & Chips $18.75 + The Standard burger $17.50 (adults) + Kids Cheeseburger $10 + Kids Fish & Fries $10 (kids, from the confirmed printed Kids menu, drink included). Entrees only. Subtotal $56.25 + Seattle 10.55% tax ($5.94) + 20% tip on subtotal ($11.25) = $73.44 total.

blocks.title: Walk back to Grand Hyatt Seattle
Breakfast — Grand Hyatt Seattle (included)
blocks.detail: Globalist complimentary breakfast, before checkout.

blocks.title: Pack up & check out — Grand Hyatt Seattle
blocks.detail: A relaxed, unhurried morning — no need to leave before 9:30.

blocks.title: Load the car from the hotel garage
Rest stop
blocks.detail: Bathroom break, grab a snack for the road.

blocks.title: Drive — over Stevens Pass to Leavenworth
blocks.detail: ~1h15m at a mountain-driving pace. Traction tires required by WSDOT policy in winter conditions — confirm the Rogue is equipped with the Turo host before the trip. Check WSDOT's live pass page morning-of for chain-up advisories or closures.

blocks.title: Arrive Hampton Inn Leavenworth — hold luggage at the desk
blocks.detail: Confirmed check-in is 3:00 PM (confirmation #97935855, 301 Ward Strasse, (509) 470-9798) -- early check-in isn't guaranteed, so bags get held at the desk rather than counting on room access.

blocks.title: Walk to Colchuck's (formerly Bavarian Bistro & Bar)
Lunch — Colchuck's
blocks.detail: German pub upstairs at 801 Front St, recently rebranded from "Bavarian Bistro & Bar" to Colchuck's (same menu/ownership) — Bavarian sampler, Jaeger schnitzel, and a confirmed kids menu, swapped in for Lost Pig Cafe which had nothing kid-appropriate.
blocks.notes: Menu prices not fully itemized online (price range posted as $10-$20). Estimated 2x Jaeger Schnitzel ~$19 (adults) + 2x kids item ~$10 (kids menu confirmed to exist, exact pricing unconfirmed) = $58.00 subtotal. Flagged as approximate -- call (509) 548-5074 to confirm exact prices. Subtotal $58.00 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($4.99) + 20% tip on subtotal ($11.60) = $74.59 total.

blocks.title: Free time / wait for the room
blocks.detail: The guaranteed check-in time is 3 PM -- this covers the wait honestly rather than assuming the room is ready right after lunch. Downtown is right there if the family wants to keep browsing instead of sitting in the lobby.

blocks.title: Check in — Hampton Inn Leavenworth
blocks.detail: 5 nights (Mon-Sat), confirmation #97935855. Room: 1 King bed, Hearing Accessible, Non-Smoking, Standard Room -- a single king, not two beds. Worth calling (509) 470-9798 ahead to confirm a rollaway/sofa arrangement for a family of 4, since "Hampton Inn & Suites" doesn't guarantee this specific room has a pull-out sofa.
blocks.notes: 308,000 Hilton Honors points for the full 5-night stay (confirmed, matches the reservation exactly). No cash portion charged -- fully covered by points. Free cancellation until 11:59 PM hotel time on Dec 20, 2026 if plans change. Free hot breakfast included daily, self-parking complimentary.
blocks.flag: Single king bed booked for a family of 4 — call ahead to confirm a rollaway/sofa.

blocks.title: Evening village stroll
blocks.detail: First look at Leavenworth's Bavarian-style downtown lit up for the holidays — chocolate shops, ornament shops, window browsing. Light introduction, not an anchor activity.

blocks.title: Dinner — Visconti's, 636 Front St
blocks.detail: Italian ristorante on Front St -- swapped in for South, since South is being moved to Christmas Eve dinner instead (avoids stacking Mexican/Tex-Mex two days after Cactus in Seattle). Confirmed kids menu (listed as a 'known for' feature). Book an early table -- reviews note it skews date-night later in the evening.
blocks.notes: Lasagna Al Forno $22 + Spaghetti & Meatballs $25 (adults, confirmed current prices) + 2x kids pasta ~$12 (kids menu confirmed to exist, exact item pricing not published -- estimated). Entrees only. Subtotal $71.00 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($6.11) + 20% tip on subtotal ($14.20) = $91.31 total.

blocks.title: Free evening / bedtime routine
blocks.detail: First night in Leavenworth — kids can wind down while the adults look over tomorrow's reindeer/sleigh morning.

blocks.title: Breakfast — Hampton Inn Leavenworth (included)
blocks.detail: Free hot breakfast, included in the room rate.

blocks.title: Drive to Leavenworth Reindeer Farm
blocks.detail: 10395 Chumstick Hwy, ~1 mile from downtown.

blocks.title: Check in for the reindeer tour
Leavenworth Reindeer Farm tour
blocks.detail: Presentation plus in-enclosure feeding/petting, optional Santa photo. Both kids are almost certainly under the farm's 55" height cutoff, which routes them to "Cupid's Corner" — a separate, calmer area with a smaller herd rather than the full-herd enclosure shown in the marketing photos. Still real feeding/petting/photos, just a more contained version of the experience.
blocks.notes: "Celebrate the Season" December pricing: $60-75/person (age 3+, dynamic), used $65/person average x4 = $260. Timed-entry reservation required, online only, no walk-ins — the farm's own site warns holiday tours sell out months in advance; book by Sept/Oct 2026.

blocks.title: Goodbyes & walk back to the car
blocks.detail: Real wrap-up time -- say goodbye to the reindeer handlers, walk back across the farm to the parking area, load up. Not built into the drive block.

blocks.title: Drive to Red-Tail Canyon Farm
blocks.detail: 2.5 mi from downtown — routing between the two farms, not through downtown.

blocks.title: Arrival, warm up by the teepee fire
blocks.detail: Red-Tail's sleigh rides only depart on the hour (11 AM-3 PM) -- this shorter gap is still a comfortable wait for the 11 AM slot: check in, use the restroom, browse the farm's small gift area.

blocks.title: Sleigh ride — Red-Tail Canyon Farm
blocks.detail: 30-minute forward-facing horse-drawn sleigh ride with wool blankets, group capped at 14. Chosen over Eagle Creek Ranch for real per-child pricing and a kid-calibrated sleigh design.
blocks.notes: $20/adult x2 + $10/child (12 and under) x2 = $60, tax included. Reservations phone-only: (800) 678-4512 — book as soon as dates are locked, no online booking and no posted lead-time guidance from a small operator during holiday week.

blocks.title: Hot cocoa at the warming teepee
blocks.detail: Included with the ride.

blocks.title: Walk to München Haus
blocks.detail: Hotel to downtown, then to the restaurant.

blocks.title: Lunch — München Haus
blocks.detail: Counter-service Bavarian grill with beer-garden seating — fast, casual, all-ages.
blocks.notes: Big Bob Bratwurst $10.65 + Dirtyface Beer Wurst $11.25 (adults) + 2x Organic Beef Dog (kid-sized) $6.75 (kids). Entrees only. Subtotal $35.40 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($3.04) + 10% tip on subtotal ($3.54) = $41.98 total.

blocks.title: Village afternoon — chocolate & ornament shops, Gingerbread Factory
blocks.detail: A single unhurried stretch of village time — no second activity stacked on top of an already full morning.

blocks.title: Free time / rest at the hotel
Dinner — Baren Haus
blocks.detail: German restaurant at 901 Front St, swapped in for Kristall's (rejected -- 'doesn't look good'). Confirmed real entree pricing and a dedicated kids menu; family-friendly per reviews. 385 photos/471 reviews on Yelp.
blocks.notes: Jagerschnitzel $16.95 + Sauerbraten $19.95 (adults, confirmed current prices) + 2x kids item (chicken strips) ~$10 (kids menu confirmed to exist, exact pricing not published -- estimated). Entrees only. Subtotal $56.90 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($4.89) + 20% tip on subtotal ($11.38) = $73.17 total.

blocks.title: Evening wind-down & bedtime routine
blocks.detail: A full morning of two farm activities plus a village afternoon — a genuinely early, easy evening.

blocks.title: Drive to Leavenworth Snowmobile Tours
blocks.detail: Mountain Springs Lodge, ~14 mi north of downtown near Lake Wenatchee.

blocks.title: Suit up & safety briefing
blocks.detail: Guides provide snowmobile suits, boots, gloves, and DOT-approved helmets. Budgeting real time for a first-time family to gear up and get the safety talk before riding.

blocks.title: Guided snowmobile tour
blocks.detail: 1-hour guided ride toward the overlook of Lake Wenatchee and the Entiat Mountain Range. Both kids (5+) ride as passengers on a 2-person sled with an adult driving — neither can drive solo, since Washington operators require snowmobile drivers to be 16+.
blocks.notes: 2-person sled $149 x 2 sleds (one per adult+kid pair) = $298. 1-hour tour is rated for ages 5+. Book in advance for holiday week — this operator, like the sleigh ride and Reindeer Farm, isn't set up for same-day walk-ins during peak season.

blocks.title: Drive to Gustav's
blocks.detail: Direct from Mountain Springs Lodge back toward town — Gustav's sits on the Hwy 2 route in, no need to detour through downtown or the hotel first.

blocks.title: Lunch — Gustav's
blocks.detail: Order-counter burger/sausage pub, real menu variety, right on the Hwy 2 route back from the snowmobile/farm side of town. Kids combo items verified live on Gustav's actual ordering system (not shown on their static website menu, but real and orderable).
blocks.notes: Gustav Burger $20 + Bratwurst $15 (adults) + Kids Cheeseburger Combo $10 + Kids Chicken Strips Combo $10 (kids -- verified live via Gustav's Toast ordering system at order.gustavsleavenworth.com; these combos don't appear on the static gustavsleavenworth.com menu page, likely a site oversight, but are real and orderable). Entrees only. Subtotal $55.00 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($4.73) + 10% tip on subtotal ($5.50) = $65.23 total.

blocks.title: Free afternoon / rest at the hotel
blocks.detail: One continuous window between a big active morning and an evening tubing session — real recovery time, not fragmented into two short stops with driving in between.

blocks.title: Snack stop — pretzel & hot cocoa downtown
blocks.detail: En route to the Ski Hill; bridges the gap to a later dinner after tubing.
blocks.notes: Subtotal $20.00 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($1.72) + 10% tip on subtotal ($2.00) = $23.72 total.

blocks.title: Tickets & gear up
blocks.detail: Lt. Michael Adams Tubing Park — walk-up, no reservations. Park opens 3:30 PM on Wednesdays, so this covers the wait.

blocks.title: Tubing — Lt. Michael Adams Tubing Park
blocks.detail: $39/person flat, buys 6 runs. All riders go solo (no lap-riding); kids 8 and under need adult supervision on the hill. Sunset is ~4:30 PM this time of year, so the back half of this session is under the hill's lights, not daylight — worth setting that expectation with the kids ahead of time.
blocks.notes: $39/person x4 = $156, all ages, same-day only.

blocks.title: Changeover — dry off & warm up
blocks.detail: Dedicated block to get out of wet snow gear before heading to dinner, not squeezed into the drive.

blocks.title: Dinner — Ludwig's
blocks.detail: Authentic German biergarten on Front St with live polka/accordion music -- swapped in for Andreas Keller (rejected -- 'looks gross'). Confirmed dedicated kids menu, 601 reviews/620 photos on Yelp, described as the closest to authentic German food in town.
blocks.notes: Menu prices not itemized online (per-person range reported $30-50). Estimated 2x schnitzel-tier entree ~$24 (adults) + 2x kids item ~$11 (kids menu confirmed to exist, exact pricing unconfirmed) = $70.00 subtotal. Flagged as approximate -- call (509) 548-6625 to confirm exact prices. Subtotal $70.00 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($6.02) + 20% tip on subtotal ($14.00) = $90.02 total.

blocks.title: Blackbird Island / Waterfront Park walk
blocks.detail: Flat, packed-snow loop along the Wenatchee River, playground at Waterfront Park. Short and kid-paced — Icicle Gorge was ruled out entirely, since Forest Service plowing stops several miles short of the actual trailhead in winter.

blocks.title: Village morning — Der Sportsmann sled rental & shopping
blocks.detail: Renting 2 sleds for tomorrow's Lake Wenatchee visit from Der Sportsmann (downtown outdoor shop), plus last Christmas Eve browsing before shops potentially cut hours.
blocks.notes: ~$60 for 2 sleds for the day, confirm exact rate in store. Picking up in town rather than relying on Lake Wenatchee State Park's own Camp Store, whose confirmed regular winter hours are Friday-Sunday 10 AM-5 PM only with no separately confirmed Christmas Day hours.

blocks.title: Lunch — Yodelin Broth Co. & Beer Garden
blocks.detail: Broth/beer-garden concept on Front St (lower level), walk-ins only -- swapped in for Kristall's (rejected -- 'doesn't look good'). Confirmed open Christmas Eve via Yodelin's own posted holiday-hours event (12-8:30pm), with a real 'Cool Kids Meals' menu.
blocks.notes: The Cheeseburger $19 + PNW Wild Salmon Rice Bowl $29 (adults) + Tillamook Grilled Cheese $8 + Kids Noodle Soup $7 (kids). Entrees only. Subtotal $63.00 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($5.42) + 10% tip on subtotal ($6.30) = $74.72 total.

blocks.title: Free afternoon / rest at the hotel
blocks.detail: Many Leavenworth businesses close early or entirely on Christmas Eve. Hampton Inn has an indoor pool if the kids want it — a real option here, not just sitting in the room.

blocks.title: Christmas Eve lights & holiday stroll
blocks.detail: Leavenworth's holiday lights stay up through the season regardless of the Lighting Festival's early-December weekends — likely quieter today than festival weekends. Extended browsing time plus hot cocoa along the way, since most shops that are still open today are worth lingering in before an early close.
blocks.notes: Hot cocoa for the family while strolling. Subtotal $20.00 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($1.72) + 10% tip ($2.00) = $23.72 total.

blocks.title: Free time / rest before dinner
blocks.detail: South (the new Christmas Eve dinner pick) is downtown and walkable, so no resort drive needed -- this is just breathing room before the holiday dinner.

blocks.title: Dinner — South, 913 Front St (Christmas Eve)
blocks.detail: Mexican/Latin -- tacos, enchiladas, burritos. Moved here from Day 3 (Mon dinner) specifically so it's not stacked two days after Cactus (Tex-Mex, Seattle Day 1) -- and it happens to be one of the few Leavenworth restaurants with standard regular hours (11am-9pm) year-round, a safer Christmas Eve bet than most. Confirmed open regular hours; call (509) 888-4328 to reconfirm no holiday-hours change. Dedicated kids menu. Slightly longer seating for a Christmas Eve dinner.
blocks.notes: Mission Burrito $19 + Chicken Poblano Enchiladas $20 (adults) + Kid's Quesadilla $9 + Kid's Chicken Tacos $8 (kids). Entrees only. Subtotal $56.00 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($4.82) + 20% tip on subtotal ($11.20) = $72.02 total.

days.title: Leavenworth — Christmas Day at Lake Wenatchee State Park

blocks.title: Breakfast — Hampton Inn Leavenworth (included)
blocks.detail: Christmas morning — a slow, unhurried start before heading out.

blocks.title: Drive to Lake Wenatchee State Park
blocks.detail: ~20.7 mi / ~30 min via Hwy 2 → SR 207. Road is plowed and open in winter (8 AM-dusk).

blocks.title: Arrival, display Sno-Park permit
blocks.detail: A Sno-Park Permit is required here, not the Discover Pass — it was purchased online in advance, since in-person vendors are almost certainly closed on Christmas Day itself. Sleds are already in the car from Der Sportsmann.
blocks.notes: One-day Sno-Park permit, $25, bought online ahead of time via WA State Parks.

blocks.title: Walk to the sled hill, unload sleds
Sledding — groomed 150-ft sled hill
blocks.detail: A real 2-hour session, not a quick stop — repeat runs, breaks to warm hands by the heated warming hut. Dropped snowshoeing from the plan; sledding is the morning's actual anchor now.

blocks.title: Pack up sleds & catch breath
blocks.detail: Real wrap-up time after 2 hours of active sledding -- gather sleds, warm hands, regroup before the next stop. Not built into the walk block.

blocks.title: Lake Wenatchee shoreline walk & viewpoint
blocks.detail: Short, flat walk along the lakeshore — no gear needed, just a look at the lake and the Cascades behind it before heading back.

blocks.title: Changeover — warm up & dry off
blocks.detail: Heated bathrooms/warming hut on-site — dedicated block before the drive back, not squeezed into it.

blocks.title: Lunch — Yodelin Broth Co. & Beer Garden
blocks.detail: Second Yodelin visit of the trip, deliberately -- it's the only Leavenworth restaurant with directly-confirmed holiday hours on BOTH Christmas Eve and Christmas Day (posted event hours, not just an assumption), and unlike the previous Kristall's repeats, it's a well-reviewed spot (731 Yelp reviews) with a real, varied kids menu. Different order than the Christmas Eve visit to keep it from feeling identical.
blocks.notes: The Cheeseburger $19 + Enchantments Salad $18 (adults) + Mini Rice Bowl $5 + Tillamook Grilled Cheese $8 (kids). Entrees only. Subtotal $50.00 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($4.30) + 10% tip on subtotal ($5.00) = $59.30 total.

blocks.title: Free afternoon / rest at the hotel
blocks.detail: Almost everything in Leavenworth is closed on Christmas Day itself — a genuinely unstructured stretch, not padding. Hampton Inn's pool is an option, along with opening any small travel gifts.

blocks.title: "Christmas Dinner in the Hills" — JJ Hills Fresh Grill, Icicle Village Resort
blocks.detail: Official resort Christmas Day dinner event, 3-7:30 PM window, with a dedicated children's menu — the most solid verified Christmas Day dinner option in town.
blocks.notes: Event pricing listed as $9-45/person for 2025/26 without an itemized adult/kids split. Last fully itemized year (2022) was $35 adult / $14 kids 6-10 — used 2 adults @ ~$45 + 2 kids @ ~$14 = ~$118 as an estimate. Reservations required — call (509) 548-5807 to confirm current per-person pricing. Subtotal $118.00 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($10.15) + 20% tip on subtotal ($23.60) = $151.75 total.

blocks.title: Pack up & check out — Hampton Inn Leavenworth
Final Leavenworth village stroll
blocks.detail: Last gift-shop browsing and Gingerbread Factory stop before leaving town.

blocks.title: Lunch — München Haus
blocks.detail: Second visit, deliberately — order-counter service means no wait right before a mountain-pass drive, which matters more today than menu variety.
blocks.notes: Helga's Giant Kelbassi $10.75 + Hans' Jalapeño & Cheddar $10.50 (adults) + 2x Organic Beef Dog $6.75 (kids). Entrees only. Subtotal $34.75 + Leavenworth 8.6% tax ($2.99) + 10% tip on subtotal ($3.48) = $41.22 total.

blocks.title: Walk back to the hotel, load the car
blocks.detail: Back to the hotel lot where the Rogue's been parked since Monday.

blocks.title: Drive — Leavenworth to Monroe/Sultan area
blocks.detail: Via US-2 over Stevens Pass, ~1.5h.

blocks.title: Drive to the Turo drop-off location
blocks.detail: Same SEA-area meeting point used for pickup on Dec 19 (confirm exact address with Indrawati via the Turo app closer to the date).

blocks.title: Drop off the Turo — 2024 Nissan Rogue
blocks.detail: Unload luggage and the 2 child seats, walk-around with the host, close out the trip in the app. Deadline is 4:00 PM — this builds in a full hour of buffer for Saturday-before-Christmas traffic near SeaTac.
blocks.flag: Hard deadline: 4:00 PM. Don't let village/lunch stops upstream run long today.

blocks.title: Wait for / ride shuttle to Courtyard by Marriott Seattle SeaTac Airport
blocks.detail: Same rental-car shuttle system, in reverse — or a short rideshare if the shuttle timing doesn't line up. Confirm at check-in whether the hotel also runs its own airport-area shuttle.

blocks.title: Check in — Courtyard by Marriott Seattle SeaTac Airport
blocks.detail: No car to park this time — just check-in.
blocks.notes: 24,000 Bonvoy points for the night (confirmed, matches the reservation exactly). No cash portion charged -- fully covered by points. Free cancellation until 5:59 PM hotel time on Dec 24, 2026 if plans change. Confirm self-parking cost isn't an issue -- there's no car to park tonight, the Turo was already returned this afternoon.

blocks.title: Rest & settle in at the hotel
blocks.detail: Shorter window than usual today since the afternoon went to the Turo return — still enough time to decompress before dinner.

blocks.title: Dinner — The Bistro, Courtyard by Marriott (on-site)
blocks.detail: Early dinner tonight, on-site — tomorrow's 5 AM car drop-off means an early bedtime, so dinner moves up rather than the usual 5-6 PM window. Staying off the flatbread items given the family's pizza aversion.

blocks.title: Bedtime routine / wind down
blocks.detail: Early night — tomorrow's a 4:45 AM wake-up for the flight home.

blocks.title: Wake, get ready
blocks.detail: 45 minutes later than originally planned — no Turo drop-off to do this morning, that happened yesterday afternoon.

blocks.title: Check out — Courtyard by Marriott Seattle SeaTac Airport
Ride hotel shuttle to SEA terminal
blocks.detail: Confirm the shuttle's early-morning schedule at check-in on Dec 26 — fall back to a quick rideshare if it doesn't run this early.

blocks.title: Check bags, TSA security
blocks.detail: Domestic, family of 4 — budgeting a full 45 minutes, though lines are typically short this early.

blocks.title: Breakfast — SEA terminal (near gate)
blocks.detail: A real sit-down breakfast, not just standing around — the 5 AM drop-off leaves genuine time for this before boarding.
blocks.notes: Subtotal $40.00 + SeaTac 10.4% tax ($4.16) + 10% tip on subtotal ($4.00) = $48.16 total.

blocks.title: Wait at the gate
blocks.detail: Trimmed to a 20-min buffer (from 30) to absorb the later start -- boarding/departure times are fixed by the airline.

blocks.title: Delta Air Lines DL 506 - Nonstop - 6h2m
blocks.detail: SEA -> MIA, 4 passengers. CONFIRMED -- confirmation G4VYUR, Delta Main cabin. SEATING ISSUE: current seat map has Roque Corona, Jennifer Borges, and Roque Luis Corona together in row 27 (A/B/C), but Chloe Corona is alone across the aisle at 27D, next to an unrelated passenger in 27F with only the middle seat 27E (currently open) between them. Worth grabbing 27E now so a family member sits next to her for the 6-hour flight, or calling Delta to rearrange.
blocks.notes: Booked and confirmed (confirmation G4VYUR, ticket numbers 0062447589301-304). Actual charge: 157,200 SkyMiles (39,300/passenger) + $22.40 cash ($5.60/passenger security fee), paid on the Visa ending 6730. This corrects the earlier cart estimate of 164,800 miles -- the actual charge came in 7,600 points lower than that estimate.
blocks.seats: Roque 27A · Jenny 27B · Roque Luis 27C · Chloe 27D (alone across the aisle)
blocks.flag: Chloe is seated alone at 27D, next to a stranger in 27F. Grab open seat 27E, or call Delta to rearrange.

items.name: Sleigh ride reservation
items.note: Red-Tail Canyon Farm, phone-only booking, (800) 678-4512 — book as soon as dates are locked

items.name: Reindeer Farm reservation
items.note: timed-entry, online only — book by Sept/Oct 2026, holiday tours sell out months out

items.name: JJ Hills Christmas Dinner pricing
items.note: call (509) 548-5807 to confirm current per-person pricing, reservations required

items.name: Sno-Park permit
items.note: must be purchased ONLINE in advance — in-person vendors will likely be closed Christmas Day

items.name: Turo pickup/drop-off exact location
items.note: Reservation 59466401 lists pickup/return generically as 'SEA Seattle-Tacoma Intl Airport' -- host Indrawati will send the exact meeting address via the Turo app closer to the date, for both the Dec 19 pickup AND the Dec 26 return (now 4:00 PM, not Dec 27).

items.name: Colchuck's (formerly Bavarian Bistro & Bar) pricing
items.note: recently rebranded from Bavarian Bistro & Bar, same menu/ownership -- exact entree and kids-item prices not published online, call (509) 548-5074 to confirm before booking

items.name: Visconti's kids menu pricing
items.note: confirmed to have a kids menu (Sirved listing) but exact kids item prices not published -- call (509) 548-1213 to confirm

items.name: Baren Haus kids menu pricing
items.note: kids menu confirmed (chicken strips, pizza sandwiches) but exact prices not published -- call ahead to confirm

items.name: Ludwig's pricing
items.note: menu prices not itemized online (per-person range reported $30-50) -- call (509) 548-6625 to confirm before booking

items.name: South Christmas Eve hours
items.note: posted regular hours are 11am-9pm year-round with no reduced-hours pattern found, but no Christmas-Eve-specific posting like Yodelin's either -- call (509) 888-4328 to reconfirm closer to the trip

items.name: Yodelin Christmas Eve/Day holiday hours (2026)
items.note: confirmed open both days via Yodelin's own posted holiday-hours events, but those postings were from the 2025 season -- reconfirm the 2026 holiday hours repeat closer to December

items.name: Turo snow chains
items.note: Not included in reservation 59466401 (AWD + snow tires only). Available as a $35/trip add-on through the host, or bring/rent your own. Dec 21 falls in Stevens Pass's historically snowiest week -- worth deciding before the trip rather than at the pass.

items.name: Courtyard SeaTac early-morning shuttle
items.note: Confirm the hotel's ~5:30 AM airport shuttle schedule at check-in on Dec 26 -- there's no car for the Dec 27 airport run anymore since the Turo is returned the afternoon before. Have a rideshare fallback in mind if the shuttle doesn't run that early.

items.name: Bainbridge Island ferry sailing times (Dec 20, 2026)
items.note: Washington State Ferries' Winter 2026-27 Seattle–Bainbridge schedule isn't published yet as of July 2026 (WSF typically posts seasonal schedules only a few months ahead). Planned around the route's typical daytime frequency (~every 40-50 min). Reconfirm exact departure times on wsdot.com/ferries once the winter schedule posts, likely this fall.

items.name: Cashmere / Aplets & Cotlets
items.note: 10 min from Leavenworth — optional half-day add-on, not included given the trip is already at 9 days

items.name: Stevens Pass ski resort
items.note: skipped — matches the no-ski-lessons pattern from prior trips unless someone already skis

items.name: Lake Chelan
items.note: recommended against — quiet/off-season in December, not worth the ~1hr detour

items.name: Downtown Leavenworth outdoor ice rink
items.note: unreliable — the marketed rink appears to still be a proposed, not-yet-built project as of Sept 2025 city council records; not built into the plan
